title: Lore Unified Settings
description: User-config schema for ~/.lore/config/settings.json. Authoritative shape for valid keys, types, and value constraints across the bash/Python/Go loaders. Closed-set rejection at the boundary: unknown frameworks, role ids, capability ids, and tui layouts must be rejected — never silently routed to a default. Migration provenance lives in ~/.lore/.install-state/migration.json (D4) and MUST NOT appear inside settings.json — additionalProperties:false on every object enforces this implicitly. Cross-field invariants (e.g. tui_launch_framework value must be present in harnesses) live in the loaders and `lore doctor`, not here. The tui_launch_framework enum and harnesses keyset MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/capabilities.json frameworks (covered by tests/frameworks/settings_schema_keyset_parity.bats); $defs/role_id MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/roles.json (same test).

version.description: Schema/file version. Increment when a breaking shape change ships. install.sh consults this against its cleanup-version constant before deleting the legacy fragmented files (D4 phase 3).

tui_launch_framework.description: Framework the TUI launches for chat/spec/followup sessions. This is a TUI-only preference; shell helpers must not treat it as the current process framework.

harnesses.description: Per-harness configuration. Keyset is closed against adapters/capabilities.json frameworks — adding a new harness requires schema bump + capabilities.json entry + install.sh allowlist update (no defaults, no fall-through).

capability_overrides.description: Operator overrides for capability support levels (D8). Keyset is closed against adapters/capabilities.json `capabilities` map — values must be one of full|partial|fallback|none. Runtime resolution applies overrides to the process-local framework selected by LORE_FRAMEWORK or harness runtime markers; the TUI launch preference does not participate in shell routing.

tui.description: TUI preferences. Persisted only in unified settings.

layout.description: Pane layout. Closed-set against tui/internal/config/config.go LayoutMode (LayoutLeftRight/LayoutTopBottom).

obsidian.description: Obsidian vault mirror configuration. Multi-vault keyed shape: each entry under `vaults` binds one knowledge base to one vault. The map key is the relative path under `$LORE_DATA_DIR/repos/` (i.e. `resolve-repo.sh` output minus the prefix), so the same identifier the install-base enumeration uses is what addresses a vault binding here. Per D-multi-vault: `enabled: false` retains `vault_path` so re-enable is a single toggle (mirrors `agent.enabled`); a missing `obsidian.vaults.<kb-key>` entry is the canonical 'sync disabled for this KB' state.

enabled.description: Per-KB sync toggle. Absence is treated as `true` for forward-compat — adding `enabled` to an entry is opt-in disable, not opt-in enable.

vault_path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the Obsidian vault directory. Empty string is REJECTED — its semantics are ambiguous (suppress vs. inherit) and doctor flags it as malformed.

vaults.description: Map of knowledge-base key → vault binding. Keyset is open at the schema layer (key shape matches the KB-relative-path produced by `resolve-repo.sh`); doctor cross-references against the install-base.

settlement.description: Durable settlement queue/processor controls. Defaults are fail-closed in readers when absent: enabled=false, max_concurrency=1, finite daily job/runtime caps. Harness selection is per job via LORE_FRAMEWORK and must not mutate tui_launch_framework.

framework_id.description: Closed framework registry. MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/capabilities.json `frameworks` keyset — drift is caught by tests/frameworks/settings_schema_keyset_parity.bats.

role_id.description: Closed role registry. MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/roles.json `roles[].id` — drift is caught by tests/frameworks/settings_schema_keyset_parity.bats. Adding a role requires updating roles.json AND this enum AND scripts/lib.sh resolve_model_for_role AND tui/internal/config/config.go ResolveModelForRole AND tests/frameworks/roles.bats.

role_value.description: Model id bound to a role (e.g. "opus", "sonnet", "haiku", or a fully-qualified provider/model like "anthropic/sonnet"). Codex also accepts a reasoning-effort suffix such as "gpt-5.5-high", split by the Codex adapter into model "gpt-5.5" plus reasoning_effort "high". Empty string is REJECTED — its semantics are ambiguous (suppress vs. use-default) and it would route through resolve_model_for_role as a non-empty truthy value in jq. The schema forbids it; doctor surfaces violations.

skill_id.description: Kebab-case skill id (e.g. "spec-design", "pr-review", "codex-plan-review"). Open at the schema layer — closed-set rejection against adapters/capabilities.json `skills` map happens in the loaders and doctor, not here. Pattern blocks obvious typos and uppercase/whitespace mistakes.

advisor_id.description: Kebab-case advisor id (e.g. "codex-design-review", "codex-plan-review", "codex-pr-review"). Open at the schema layer — closed-set rejection against the skills registry happens in the loaders and doctor.

roles_map_overlay.description: Per-harness role -> model bindings. Closed-keyset against $defs/role_id. All keys optional except where callers need a concrete default.

ceremonies_map.description: Skill -> advisor list. Property keys are kebab-case skill ids; values are arrays of kebab-case advisor ids. Empty array is valid and means "run with no advisors". Absent key means no ceremony configured for this harness. Keyset is open at the schema layer; closed-set rejection against the skills registry lives in the loaders and doctor.

additionalProperties.description: Empty array = explicit no-advisors binding. Non-empty = explicit advisor list. Absent key = no ceremony configured for this harness.

harness_block.description: Per-harness configuration block. `args` is required (every harness has a CLI arg list, possibly empty). New settings should include `roles` and `ceremonies` here because agent defaults and ceremony skills vary by harness. Absent `enabled` is default-on; explicit `false` disables this harness's integration.

args.description: CLI args prepended to every harness invocation. Empty array is valid (no flags). For claude-code the legacy default was ["--dangerously-skip-permissions"]; other harnesses default to [].

enabled.description: Per-harness integration toggle. When false, scripts/harness-toggle/disable.sh has removed this harness's skill/agent symlinks and cleared its instruction-file lore region. Absent = enabled. Mutated only via `lore harness {enable,disable}`; direct settings.json patches that bypass the toggle scripts are rejected by the unified loaders to keep symlink/instruction-file state in sync with the flag.

capability_id.description: Closed capability registry. MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/capabilities.json `capabilities` map — drift is caught by tests/frameworks/settings_schema_keyset_parity.bats.

capability_overrides_map.description: Capability -> support-level overrides. Closed-keyset against $defs/capability_id. Empty object is the default (no overrides).

enabled.description: Global dispatch gate. Readers default to false when absent.

batch_size.description: Maximum pending items in the recomputed active batch, and the maximum number of backlog rows scored per recompute.

batch_recompute_min_interval_seconds.description: Minimum seconds between automatic process-once/manual queue recomputes; zero disables throttling.

concordance_window_size.description: Requested relevance concordance window. Readers cap the effective window to the active batch size.

max_auto_retry_attempts.description: Attempts bound for auto-retrying infrastructure-failure audit runs (judge non-JSON, executor timeout, unresolved artifact), counted as non-invalidated infra-failure run records per item. Below the cap an item auto-readmits to recompute; at the cap it becomes exhausted and is excluded until `lore settlement retry-errors` resets it. Zero reproduces the pre-auto-retry behavior: any single infra failure excludes the item.

lease_ttl_seconds.description: Expired active leases are reclaimed to pending once under the settlement lock.

executor_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum duration for an external settlement executor.

auditor_model.description: Judge model for settlement-executed audits, passed verbatim to the harness (exported as LORE_MODEL_JUDGE by settlement-audit-executor.sh). Free text so dated model ids work; per-harness tier aliases live in capabilities.json model_routing.tiers. Unset means the judge role's normal resolution applies — express unset by omitting the key, never by an empty string.

enabled.description: When enabled, settlement dispatches only during the configured local time window.

timezone.description: IANA timezone name such as America/New_York, or local for the machine timezone.

ranges.description: Active dispatch windows. Each range has selected days plus local start/end times; overnight ranges are allowed. When unset, settlement may dispatch at all times.

census_enabled.description: Dispatch posture. False (default) is event-driven: enqueue happens only via `lore settlement triggers` (dispute detector, spot-sample budget, rollup steady-state) and explicit enqueue; batch recompute never refills from the source-stream backlog. True re-enables the dormant census wholesale (scan-era recompute refill and the TUI auto-process backlog arm).

spot_sample_weekly_budget.description: Spot-sample audits enqueued per ISO week. Starts high per the settlement disposition memo — a throttled census while peer-verification volume is unproven; ratchet down as lore-verify volume proves out, hold or raise if thin.

mode.description: Per-job harness selection. random samples only from eligible frameworks; active uses the process-local LORE_FRAMEWORK value (defaulting to claude-code) and rejects when that framework is ineligible.

eligible_frameworks.description: Empty means all registered frameworks are candidates before global harness enablement, support, and evidence gates.
